findings.title: Curve near-par minimum output plus swallowed divest reverts can permissionlessly DoS strategy exits

best_agent_match.title: Curve price skew or stETH depeg can permissionlessly DoS all strategy exits

findings.title: Balancer flash-loan fees are ignored, so any nonzero fee bricks flash-loan-dependent flows

best_agent_match.title: Balancer flash-loan fees are ignored, so any nonzero fee bricks the strategy

findings.title: `totalLockedValue()` underflows instead of flooring at zero, blocking distressed-strategy recovery flows

best_agent_match.title: A slightly underwater Aave position makes `totalLockedValue()` revert and bricks vault recovery flows

findings.title: TVL and divest sizing treat stETH collateral as if it exits at par, overstating withdrawable WETH during discounts

best_agent_match.title: TVL and divest math assume 1 stETH = 1 ETH, overstating real withdrawable value

findings.title: `createAaveDebt()` authorizes any active strategy, enabling cross-strategy debt siphoning if another strategy is compromised

best_agent_match.title: createAaveDebt can be called by any strategy without proper validation

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked WSTETH wrapping return value allows loss of funds
rejected_candidates.reason: The low-level payable call is followed by `require(success, ...)`, so the wrap path does not fail silently. The candidate also ignores that mainnet wstETH's payable entrypoint is intentionally used for ETH staking/wrapping.

rejected_candidates.title: No slippage protection when wrapping ETH to WSTETH
rejected_candidates.reason: Wrapping/staking into wstETH is not a market trade against external liquidity; it follows Lido's deterministic conversion rate. This is not a realistic slippage bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Incorrect slippage calculation in _endPosition uses STETH balance instead of expected ETH
rejected_candidates.reason: The important bug is the opposite of the candidate's claim: the code is over-strict and near-par, causing reverts/DoS, not permissive low-min-output value leakage. That issue is merged into F-001.

rejected_candidates.title: Strategy reports more liquidated WETH than it actually transfers back to the vault
rejected_candidates.reason: `_divest()` can over-report `assetsReceived`, but in the in-scope code this only pollutes events and `_liquidate()`'s returned accounting value. No visible withdrawal or settlement path releases extra assets based on that inflated number.

rejected_candidates.title: Unbounded array in updateStrategyAllocations allows gas griefing
rejected_candidates.reason: `updateStrategyAllocations()` is restricted to `HARVESTER`. A privileged caller can always waste its own gas; there is no permissionless griefing vector here.

rejected_candidates.title: setSlippageBps lacks proper access control and validation
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a strategist-controlled risk parameter. Missing bounds may permit operator error, but not a permissionless or trust-boundary-breaking vulnerability in the current model.

rejected_candidates.title: setBorrowBps lacks bounds validation allowing excessive leverage
rejected_candidates.reason: This is also a strategist-controlled configuration knob. Abuse requires trusted-role misconfiguration or compromise, which is outside normal reportable scope here.

rejected_candidates.title: Rebalance can be called by any harvester without proper sequencing
rejected_candidates.reason: `HARVESTER` is an explicitly privileged role. The candidate does not identify a concrete state corruption or fund-loss path beyond normal privileged misuse.

rejected_candidates.title: No slippage protection in Curve exchange in _rebalancePosition
rejected_candidates.reason: The rebalance path does have a minimum output check (`min_dy = ethBorrowed`). The real problem is that this threshold is again near-par and can revert during discounts, which is already captured by F-001.

rejected_candidates.title: Sweep function can extract any ERC20 from strategy including asset tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: `sweep()` is governance-only. Governance can already fully control strategy allocation and removal, so this is a trust-model concern rather than a distinct vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Harvest can be called repeatedly to grief profit locking
rejected_candidates.reason: The described issue is just competition among privileged harvesters for who submits the permitted transaction. It does not create protocol insolvency, theft, lockup, or meaningful DoS.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing reentrancy guard in receiveFlashLoan callback
rejected_candidates.reason: The candidate is speculative and does not show a concrete reentrant call path that can violate invariants in the current code. External integrations alone do not make this reportable.

rejected_candidates.title: No slippage protection on AAVE deposit in _addToPosition
rejected_candidates.reason: Aave deposits mint aTokens deterministically against the deposited amount; this is not an AMM trade and does not need swap-style slippage checks.

rejected_candidates.title: Strategy upgrade lacks deadline allowing stuck upgrades
rejected_candidates.reason: If the upgrade handshake fails, the Balancer flash loan reverts the entire transaction atomically. There is no partial stuck state from a missing deadline in the shown flow.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential integer overflow in _increaseTVLBps
rejected_candidates.reason: Solidity 0.8.x checked arithmetic already reverts on overflow, so the claimed bug does not exist in this codebase.

rejected_candidates.title: Fixed slippageBps value may be insufficient during market stress
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a weaker restatement of the concrete exit-DoS issue already captured in F-001, not a separate vulnerability.
